title: Capaciteitsmanager Business Clients & Ecosystems
description:
In het kort
Ben jij die ervaren Capaciteitsmanager die workforce management binnen onze afdeling naar een hoger niveau wil brengen? Ben je een ervaren WFM-er, slim, ambitieus, analytisch sterk en communicatief vaardig?  Weet jij als geen ander hoe je interne stakeholders moet overtuigen van jouw adviezen en bevindingen? Dan gaan wij graag met jou in gesprek!
Je werk
Je werkt in een klein WFM team en bent daar verantwoordelijk voor de WFM cyclus en het forecastproces bij Business Clients. Je maakt de forecast voor alle werkstromen die met name via Microsoft Dynamics lopen en onderzoekt hoe we in de toekomst alle werkzaamheden van de business kunnen forcecasten en inplannen via WFM. Je stelt een capaciteitsplan op en geeft het management aan de hand hiervan advies over benodigde capaciteit en werkverdeling. De uitkomsten van het capaciteitsoverleg draag je over aan de Planners en Traffic managers. Je levert een proactieve bijdrage aan de inzet en optimalisatie van WFM. Je rapporteert aan de manager WFM BC&E. Het betreft een fulltime functie.
Werkomgeving
Bij het Centre of Expertise (CoE) WFM zijn alle WFM experts binnen de bank ondergebracht die voor verschillende afdelingen WFM neerzetten en uitvoeren. Je komt te werken in het executie team dat verantwoordelijk is voor WFM bij Business Clients & Ecosystems (BC&E).
Je standplaats wordt de Wisselwerking 58 in Diemen maar bij het Centre of Expertise WFM werken wij hybride, hierbij bepalen de werkzaamheden wat voor jou die dag de beste werkplek is.
Business Clients & Ecosystems
De client unit Business Clients & Ecosystems (BC&E) is verantwoordelijk voor het bedienen van zakelijke klanten met een omzet tot 25 miljoen Euro, ingedeeld in 'sectorclusters'. De focus van BC&E ligt op het behalen van onze groeidoelstelling om de nummer één bank voor het midden- en kleinbedrijf  (zakelijke klanten met omzet tot 25 miljoen) te worden.
BC&E bedient +/- 370.000 zakelijke klanten (inclusief Medical Business Clients). De afdeling adviseert zakelijke klanten over dagelijkse zakelijke producten en diensten, zoals leningen tot een miljoen Euro, betaaloplossingen, sparen, verzekeringen, beyond en near banking proposities en ondersteuning bij het dagelijks bankieren.
Je profiel
Meerdere jaren werkervaring op het gebied van Workforce management (WFM); Ervaring met het implementeren van WFM bij een nieuwe afdeling is een pre.
Analytisch sterk en communicatief vaardig.
Kennis en ervaring met PowerBI rapportages is een pre.
Wij bieden
Het is voor ons belangrijk om een goede werkgever te zijn. Wij bieden je daarom een functie met impact én uitstekende arbeidsvoorwaarden die bijdragen aan jouw werkplezier, ontwikkeling en wellbeing.
Daarnaast bieden wij:
Een aantrekkelijk bruto maandsalaris op basis van een 36urige werkweek. Dit is inclusief vakantiegeld en een flexibel benefit budget.
Een uitstekende pensioenregeling, zodat je goed voorbereid bent voor later.
Flexibiliteit in werken: thuiswerken is mogelijk in overleg met je team en afhankelijk van je functie. Wij regelen een ergonomische thuiswerkplek voor je.
Ruimte voor ontspanning met vijf weken vakantie per jaar, aangevuld met twee verplichte vrije dagen. Daarnaast kun je jaarlijks tot vier extra weken vakantie bijkopen.
Vijf ‘Banking for better days’: extra vrije dagen die je kunt inzetten voor persoonlijke ontwikkeling of vrijwilligerswerk.
Persoonlijke ontwikkeling staat centraal: je ontvangt een ontwikkelbudget van €1.000 per jaar, dat je kunt opsparen tot €3.000.
Een OV-jaarabonnement met gratis openbaar vervoer in heel Nederland voor zakelijk en privé gebruik.
Interesse?
Heb je interesse? Reageer dan door middel van een sollicitatie. Voor extra inhoudelijke info graag even reageren naar
thomas.van.hassel@nl.abnamro.com
/ 06 1439 7379.
Jouw Toekomst: Inclusief, Innovatief en Duurzaam
Bij ABN AMRO geloven we in "
Banking for better, for generations to come.
" Gelijke kansen voor iedereen vormen een belangrijk fundament, waarbij we streven naar een inclusieve cultuur waarin alle medewerkers zich gezien, gehoord en gewaardeerd voelen. Onze visie als
persoonlijke bank in de digitale tijd
sluit naadloos aan bij de behoefte aan verrassende inzichten en innovatieve oplossingen, voortkomend uit een divers samenspel van culturen en ervaringen. We richten ons op klantbeleving, duurzaamheid en bouwen aan een toekomstbestendige bank, waarbij we gelijke beloning voor gelijk werk jaarlijks onderzoeken om eerlijke kansen te waarborgen. Sluit je aan bij een bank die vindingrijkheid en ambitie omarmt, en maak samen met ons impact voor een betere toekomst.

title: Senior Data Analyst
description:
At a glance
Are you passionate about both advanced analytics and empowering others to grow in their analytical capabilities? Do you have the expertise to lead by example while fostering a culture of statistical thinking? If so, we have the perfect role for you!
Channel Security is seeking a Lead Data Analyst to join our team; Fraud Monitoring. In this role, you will not only be a technical expert and a strong individual contributor but also a key leader in advancing the analytics maturity of a team with a business-driven background. You will serve as a pillar for analytics standards and help shape the way we use data to make impactful decisions.
Your job
As a Lead Data Analyst within the Fraud Monitoring team, you will play a central role in ensuring that our analysis and detection logic are grounded in solid reasoning and supported by evidence. You will work closely with Fraud Experts (FEs), Data Scientists, and Machine Learning Engineers to strengthen our fraud detection capabilities across payment channels to catch frauds such as phishing, account takeover, lost or stolen cards, and other emerging threats.
Your work focuses on deep‑dive analysis, understanding fraud behaviour, evaluating the effectiveness of detection logic, and validating whether proposed changes are analytically sound. You will help the team distinguish clear signals from noise, recognise when evidence is insufficient, and ensure our decisions are based on robust data rather than anecdotal cases.
This is a hands‑on analytical role with significant influence: your insights and your methodological judgment will directly shape how we detect fraud and protect our customers.
Key Responsibilities:
Analytical Excellence
Perform in‑depth analyses to understand fraud patterns, attack vectors, seasonality, behavioural shifts, and detection performance across payment channels.
Evaluate the strength of proposed detection logic (features, thresholds, rules) and identify when ideas are overly narrow, unsupported by evidence, or prone to false positives.
Assess the value and reliability of data fields and signals used in fraud detection.
Help ensure our reasoning, conclusions, and decisions are analytically sound and well‑supported.
Methodological Leadership
Guide Fraud Experts and junior analysts in applying good analytical practices and critical thinking.
Coach colleagues on interpreting data responsibly, avoiding common analytical pitfalls, and understanding basic statistical concepts such as variance, sample bias, and signal strength.
Promote a culture of analytical rigor — ensuring the team adopts more mature, data‑driven ways of working.
Collaboration & Impact
Partner with Data Scientists to transform analytical insights into hypotheses, experiments, and feature ideas for model development.
Act as a bridge between domain experts and technical experts, ensuring clear understanding on both sides.
Identify opportunities to improve how we measure fraud impact, effectiveness of controls, and operational efficiency.
Drive improvements in how we use data in the team — from investigation workflows to decision support tools — focusing on quality, clarity, and insight rather than dashboard volume.
Working environment
Grid Channel Security within Corporate Information Security Office (CISO) is responsible for the prevention and detection of payment fraud, and deciding the policy and strategy in the area of fraud. The Fraud Monitoring team that you are going to be part of, is a motivated multidisciplinary group of approximately 25 colleagues working with a focus on protecting customers. We work in a hybrid way and our day to day work is organized by the agile and scrum principles. CISO is recognised as having an informal work atmosphere where everyone has a heart for their job and is prepared to help others.
Your profile
Qualifications and technical skills:
A master’s degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Computer Science, Statistics, Econometrics, Engineering, Physics, Mathematics, or similar).
At least 3 years of experience in an analytical role involving hands-on data exploration, problem solving, and interpretation (e.g., data analyst, fraud analyst, risk analyst, business analyst with strong technical skills, data scientists in analytical track).
Technical skills:
Strong SQL skills for exploratory analysis and investigation.
Hands-on experience in Python for data analysis.
PySpark or experience working with large datasets.
Basic familiarity with visualization tools for communication — not for dashboard engineering.
Ability to frame analytical questions, interpret results correctly, and identify flawed logic or insufficient evidence.
Familiarity with basic statistical concepts (e.g., distributions, variance, seasonality, sampling, simple backtesting principles). Not as a statistician— but as a critical thinker.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.
Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
About you
You are an energetic Firestarter, proactively looking for and finding relevant data analysis questions
Strong critical thinking and the confidence to challenge weak assumptions.
Ability to clearly articulate reasoning, uncertainties, and trade-offs.
A Teacher and Mentor with a deep passion for upskilling and helping your fellow team members grow their analytical capabilities, especially guiding them to adopt statistical rigor during their analytical work.
Experience and understanding of Scrum and Agile methodologies, with the ability to apply these frameworks effectively in project management and team collaboration
We are offering
Flexibility in a hybrid work environment to support strong work-life balance;
An industry-competitive salary based on a 36 or 40-hour work week;
A supplementary benefit budget of 11%, which you can spend on pre-tax fringe benefits;
A personal development budget of EUR 1.000 per year;
An annual public transportation pass;
A solid pension plan.
Interested?
Does this opportunity sound like something you’d be interested in? Please send us your application as soon as possible. We look forward to meeting you.
Please note: This vacancy is a repost of a position previously published on 27 November 2025; if you have already received a rejection for this role, reapplying will not be considered.
Your Future: Inclusive, Innovative, Sustainable
At ABN AMRO, we believe in "
Banking for better, for generations to come.
" Equal opportunities for everyone are a crucial foundation, as we strive for an inclusive culture where all employees feel seen, heard, and valued. Our vision of
being a personal bank in the digital age
aligns perfectly with the demand for surprising insights and innovative solutions, born from a diverse interplay of cultures and experiences. We focus on customer experience, sustainability, and building a future-proof bank, conducting annual reviews to ensure equal pay for equal work. Join a bank that embraces ingenuity and ambition, and make an impact with us for a better future.
If you want to apply for the B-Able or Reboot program, make sure to mention it both in the title and the content of your resume.

title: Data Analyst - Transaction Banking
description:
In het kort
Ben jij een
Data Analist
met een passie voor de financiële sector en ben je enthousiast om meer te leren over verschillende bancaire producten? Wil jij deel uitmaken van een jong en zeer gemotiveerd team vol met Data Professionals die het vertrouwen krijgen om data in te zetten voor strategische, operationele, en tactische besluitvorming? Klinkt het idee van een autonoom team met veel vrijheid om te leren, te groeien en impact te maken interessant? Bij het BI & Analytics Center of Expertise ben je aan het juiste adres!
Werkomgeving
De Products Data Practice Unit is onderdeel van de Data Domains: Accounts, Savings & Transactions, and Insurances (AST&I). Momenteel worden deze Data Domains opgezet binnen de bankbrede implementatie van het Federated Data Governance Model. De AST&I Data Domains vallen binnen de Transaction Banking productunit, die een uitgebreid portfolio van producten omvat: betaal- en spaarrekeningen, betaalkaarten, productpakketten, verzekeringen, open banking API's, trade finance, digitale producten zoals Apple Pay/Google Pay, en bijbehorende sales-, business- & risicomanagementprocessen. Wij dragen voor een significant deel bij aan de inkomsten van de bank, beheren verschillende regelgevende en operationele onderwerpen, en dragen bij aan de Objectives & Key Results die zijn vastgesteld voor de Personal & Business Banking Client Unit, waarmee we ongeveer 6 miljoen Personal & Business Banking klanten bedienen.
De Products Data Practice afdeling bestaat uit twee Centers of Expertise: 1) Het BI & Analytics CoE (BI&A CoE), een team van data-analisten verantwoordelijk voor BI & Reporting en Data Analytics, en 2) Het Advanced Analytics & AI Center of Expertise (AA&AI CoE), een team van Data Scientists verantwoordelijk voor het portfolio van AA&AI use cases in lijn met de strategie van de AST&I Data Domains. Daarnaast is Products Data Practice verantwoordelijk voor ons cloud-based data- en analyticsplatform, de Transaction Banking Domain Data Store (DDS).
De visie van het team is om een omgeving te stimuleren waarin data essentieel is voor alles wat we doen. Wij passen geavanceerde analyses en opkomende technologieën toe om waardevolle inzichten te verkennen, onderzoeken en verzamelen; we definiëren, rapporteren & monitoren en verbeteren de prestaties voor verschillende productgroepen; adviseren hen over nieuwe voorstelontwerpen en duty of care-onderwerpen; voorspellen en prognosticeren trends in het gebruik van producten; helpen bij het bepalen van de koers en het stellen van prioriteiten om grote klanttevredenheid en leveranciersrelaties te bereiken. We fungeren ook als veranderaars om een data-gedreven mindset op alle niveaus van de organisatie te verankeren, waardoor ze het volledige potentieel van de data die we hebben kunnen benutten en daarmee de ontwikkeling & kennis van data binnen de bank versterken.
Al onze Data Analysts en Scientists hebben een T-profiel en naast Data Analysts & Scientists kunnen ze verschillende rollen vervullen zoals: Lead Analysts, Subject Matter Experts, Business Liaisons, etc. Products Data Practice telt meer dan 25 werknemers (65% Analysts & Scientists + 35% Engineers & Modelers) en bestaat uit 7 nationaliteiten die gevestigd zijn in Nederland en India. We volgen agile werkmethodes en werken aan cross-grid en cross-functionele initiatieven in samenwerking met andere data teams, Business Experts, Business Developers, Product Owners, Seniors en leiders – waardoor er een uitstekende mogelijkheid ontstaat om te netwerken en de omgeving dienst als een kenniscentrum. We hebben een cultuur ontwikkeld waarin ondernemerschap, empathie, plezier en het vieren van successen echt worden nageleefd. Dit doen we door de values van de bank te respecteren en na te leven: Care, Courage en Collaboration!
Je werk
In jouw rol als Data Analist zal je:
Fungeren als een data-sparringpartner voor onze stakeholders voor KPIs en rapportages, met relevante inzichten voor het juiste publiek.
Onderliggende processen van verschillende producten en diensten begrijpen en zal je goed de verbanden kunnen leggen
Zowel zelfstanding als gezamenlijk met je mede-analisten aan interessante cases werken
Deelnemen aan complexe lange termijnprojecten, met focus op continious delivery en een MVP way-of-working
Bruikbare inzichten uit grote databronnen halen en voer je periodieke/ad hoc kwantitatieve analyses en rapportages uit
Data-onderzoek/-analyse uitvoeren op verschillende datasets (producten, transacties, kanalen, klanten)
Samenwerken met multidisciplinaire teams en promoot je teams die samenwerken op verschillende niveaus
De kunst van storytelling optimaliseren door middel van cijfers en effectieve visualisatietechnieken
Analytisch en georganiseerd te werk en heb je zowel een oog voor detail als voor het grotere geheel
Gefocust op continue verbetering en het automatiseren van huidige processen
Nieuwsgierig te werk, stel je voldoende vragen om dieper in de stof te duiken en aarzel je niet om de status quo uit te dagen
Proactief te werk met het zoeken naar verbetermogelijkheden met betrekking tot team, processen en data
Je profiel
Denk je dat je geschikt bent? Hieronder staan de gevraagde kwalificaties:
Diploma in STEM of economie/bedrijfskunde, met een focus op analytics
3-5 jaar werkervaring, waarvan minimaal 2 jaar op analytisch gebied
Vaardigheid in programmeertalen zoals SQL of Python
Ervaring met datavisualisatie tools zoals PowerBI of Tableau
Kennis over datawarehouse-concepten en datamodelleringstechnieken
Basis kennis in Advance Analytics technieken (Forecasting, Prediction, Cluster Analysis & Machine Learning) of de motivatie om dit te ontwikkelen
Een sterke combinatie van analytische vaardigheden, intellectuele nieuwsgierigheid, communicatieve en rapportagevaardigheden
Uitstekende schriftelijke en mondelinge communicatieve vaardigheden in het Nederlands en het Engels
Nice to have: Ervaring met werken op azure/ databricks/ of andere cloud platforms
Nice to have: Kennis van de agile methodologie en het vermogen om verschillende prioriteiten gelijktijdig te managen.
Nice to have: Kennis van Data Warehouse technieken en data modellering technieken
Nice to have: Kennis van de financiële sector en/of bankproducten
Nice to have: Microsoft Certified Power BI Data Analyst Associate (PL-300)
Nice to have: Databricks Certified Data Analyst
Wij bieden
De mogelijkheid om proactief aan je vitaliteit en fitheid te werken
Een concurrerend salaris, passend bij je ervaring en kennis
Een aanvullend Benefit Budget van 11%, dat je kunt gebruiken om flexibele arbeidsvoorwaarden te kopen
Een persoonlijk ontwikkelingsbudget van EUR 1.000 per jaar
Een jaarlijks OV-abonnement of reiskostenvergoeding, afhankelijk van de functie
Een solide pensioenregeling
Interesse?
Wil je meer weten over de functie, neem dan gerust contact op met Amber (Amber.Stoll@nl.abnamro.com).
Je kunt tot en met 20 mei solliciteren. Na deze datum wordt de vacature gesloten en ontvang je binnenkort feedback op je sollicitatie.

Bij ABN AMRO geloven we in "
Banking for better, for generations to come
." Gelijke kansen voor iedereen vormen een belangrijk fundament, waarbij we streven naar een inclusieve cultuur waarin alle medewerkers zich gezien, gehoord en gewaardeerd voelen. Onze visie als persoonlijke bank in de digitale tijd sluit naadloos aan bij de behoefte aan verrassende inzichten en innovatieve oplossingen, voortkomend uit een divers samenspel van culturen en ervaringen. We richten ons op klantbeleving, duurzaamheid en bouwen aan een toekomstbestendige bank, waarbij we gelijke beloning voor gelijk werk jaarlijks onderzoeken om eerlijke kansen te waarborgen. Sluit je aan bij een bank die vindingrijkheid en ambitie omarmt, en maak samen met ons impact voor een betere toekomst.

title: Data Analyst - Transaction Banking
description:
At a glance
Are you a
Data Analyst
, who is fascinated by the financial sector and eager to explore the world of banking products & transaction data? Do you want to be part of a team of young and highly motivated Data Professionals, who are entrusted and empowered to make use of data for strategic, regulatory, operational, and tactical decision making? Does the idea of an autonomous team with the freedom to learn, grow and make impact sound exciting? The BI & Analytics Center of Expertise is this and more!
Working environment
We are the Product Data Practice department, part of the Data Domains of Accounts, Savings & Transactions, and Insurances (AST&I), currently being set up by the implementation of the bank’s federated data governance model. The AST&I Data Domains are within the Transaction Banking product unit, which covers a vast portfolio of products: accounts, payment cards, packages, savings, insurances, open banking APIs, trade finance, digital products like Apple Pay/ Google Pay, and associated sales, business & risk management processes. We contribute significantly to the revenue of the bank, manage different regulatory and operational topics, and contribute to Objectives and Key Results set for the Personal & Business Banking Client Unit, serving about 6 million Personal & Business Banking customers.
The Products Data Practice department is composed by two Centers of Expertise: 1) The BI & Analytics CoE (BI&A CoE), a team of data analysts responsible for the BI & Reporting and Data Analytics, and 2) The Advanced Analytics & AI Center of Expertise (AA&AI CoE), a team of Data Scientists responsible for the  portfolio of AA&AI use cases in line with the strategy of the AST&I Data Domains and the bank-wide AI Strategy. In addition, the Products Data Practice department is responsible for our cloud-based data and analytics platform with the Transaction Banking Domain Data Store (DDS).
The team’s vision is to stimulate an environment where data is vital to everything we do.
We apply analytics and emerging technologies to explore, research and gather valuable insights; we define, report & monitor and improve performance for several product groups; advise them on new proposition designs and duty of care topics; predict and forecast trends of product’s usage; help decide the course of action and setting priorities to achieve great customer satisfaction and vendor relations. We also act as change agents to embed a data driven mindset at all levels of the organization enabling them to get the full potential of the data that we have, thereby strengthening data maturity and literacy in the bank.
All our Data Analysts and Scientists have T-shaped profiles and next to data analytics and data science they can play different roles like: Lead Analysts, Subject Matter Experts, Business Liaisons etc. The Products Data Practice is 25+ people strong (65% Analysts & Scientists + 35% Engineers & modelers) and consists of 7 nationalities which are based across the Netherlands and India. We follow agile principles and work on cross grid and cross functional initiatives while collaborating with other analytics teams, business experts, business developers, product owners, seniors, and leaders – hence providing an excellent opportunity for networking and serving as a knowledge haven. We have developed a culture where entrepreneurial spirit, empathy, element of fun and celebrating successes is truly appreciated. We do this by respecting and living our bank’s values: Care, Courage, and Collaboration!
Your job
In your role as a Reporting Data Analyst, you will:
Act as a data sparring partner with business stakeholders on smart KPIs & reports, with relevant insights for the right audience
Understand underlying processes for different products & services, and connect the dots
Work both in individual contributor roles (leading project) and in collaboration / co-creating with multiple analysts
Engage in complex long term projects, with focus on continuous delivery in small increments
Extract actionable insights from large data sources, perform recurring or ad hoc analyses / reports
Can conduct data research/analytics on different datasets (products, transactions, channels, customers, CRM/ sales)
Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams and manage different stakeholders
Practice the art of storytelling through numbers and effective visualization techniques
Organize your work in a structured manner and effectively communicate to all parties involved
Focus on continuous improvement and automation of existing deliverables
Be curious, ask enough questions to deep dive and not hesitate to change/ challenge the status quo
Remain proactive in coming up with ideas to improve/ solve (technical/ business/ data) challenges
Your profile
Do you think you are a fit? Check your qualifications:
Degree in STEM or economics/business, with an analytics focus
2-5 years of work experience, with at least 2 years in data/ analytical field
Experienced in programming language such as SQL and Python
Experience with data visualization tools like PowerBI or Tableau
Basic understanding in Advance Analytics techniques (Forecasting, Prediction, Cluster Analysis & Machine Learning) or a willingness to learn and develop in that area
A combination of analytical skills, intellectual curiosity, stakeholder management, communication, and reporting skills
Effective written and verbal communication skills in English (and Dutch)
Nice to have : Experience in working on azure/ databricks/ or similar cloud platforms
Nice to have : Knowledge of agile methodology, and ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously
Nice to have: Understanding of Data Warehouse concepts and data modelling techniques
Nice to have: Knowledge of the financial sector and/or banking products
Nice to have: Microsoft Certified Power BI Data Analyst Associate (PL-300)
Nice to have: Databricks Certified Data Analyst
We are offering
The opportunity to pro-actively work on your vitality and fitness
A competitive salary, fitting to your experience and expertise
Additional benefit budget of 11%, which you can use to buy flexible working conditions
A personal development budget of EUR 1.000 per year
An annual public transportation pass or travel budget, depending on the function
A solid pension plan
Interested?
If you want to know more about the position, feel free to reach out to Amber (Amber.Stoll@nl.abnamro.com).
You can apply until 20 May. After this date, the vacancy will close and you will receive feedback on your application shortly.

title: Software Engineering Manager – Unified Platform
description:
This is Adyen
Adyen provides payments, data, and financial products in a single solution for customers like Meta, Uber, H&M, and Microsoft — making us the financial technology platform of choice. At Adyen, everything we do is engineered for ambition.
For our teams, we create an environment with opportunities for our people to succeed, backed by the culture and support to ensure they are enabled to truly own their careers. We are motivated individuals who tackle unique technical challenges at scale and solve them as a team. Together, we deliver innovative and ethical solutions that help businesses achieve their ambitions faster.
Why you should join the Unified Platform
The
Unified Platform
powers the core financial infrastructure behind Adyen’s platform - from how transactions are processed to how financial data is stored, monitored, and configured. It plays a critical role in ensuring that payments, accounting, reporting, and reconciliation work seamlessly across all products and customer segments.
We are currently hiring multiple
Software Engineering Managers
within the Unified Platform. These teams work on different parts of the platform but share a common mission: building scalable, reliable systems that power Adyen’s global financial ecosystem.
Depending on your experience and strengths, you may join one of the following domains:
Storage & Scale
Design and evolve the storage architecture behind Adyen’s financial systems, ensuring our data infrastructure can scale reliably as transaction volumes grow.
Finance Observability
Enable transparent and reliable financial operations by building monitoring and alerting systems for critical financial flows across the platform.
Your final team placement will be determined based on your skills, experience, and the technical fit identified during the interview process.

title: Projectmanager ICT | The Hague or Groningen
description:
A powerful digital future in The Netherlands and Europe is unthinkable without research and innovation. Fortunately, TNO is bursting with exiting project plans, which, thanks to you, will actually come to fruition. As a project manager ICT at TNO, you will work at the cutting edge of applied research and pragmatic ICT solutions for the energy domain. Join our enthusiastic group of ICT specialists and take the lead in project teams. Together with companies, universities, research centres and government partners, you’ll help resolve energy transition issues faced by industry and society as a whole!
You will manage multiple research projects simultaneously, ranging from B2B consultancy assignments to multi-year, grant-funded TKI projects (Top Consortia for Knowledge and Innovation). Especially in consortium projects, stakeholder management is key due to the diverse—and sometimes conflicting—interests at play. You safeguard the shared objectives and the overall progress of the project.
Together with your multidisciplinary team, you will develop innovative proof-of-concepts and demonstrators. Your teams work on topics such as modelling of distributed, hybrid energy systems, Digital Twins, adaptive algorithms, (agentic) AI, and more.
Your focus will initially be on Dutch projects, but depending on your interests, you’ll have the chance to move on to European projects.
You will be part of the Intelligent Cyber-physical Systems Engineering (ICSE) research group. From our locations in Groningen and The Hague, more than 35 scientists, researchers, consultants and project managers demonstrate the added value of digital technologies every day. You will also work closely with colleagues across other departments and units.
ICSE offers a vibrant mix of highly educated professionals of different ages, nationalities, genders and experience levels. What unites us is our passion for applied research and our love for an open, informal working atmosphere. And at TNO you’re never on your own: we support each other, collaborate closely and welcome your ideas.
You thrive on managing multidisciplinary research projects. The inherent uncertainties of innovative projects—with outcomes yet unknown—don’t throw you off balance. Inspiring people, coming up with creative solutions and working together are second nature to you.
With your strong communication skills, you’re able to convince your team, clients, partners and other stakeholders. You navigate sensitivities and differing interests with ease. And of course, you do this within budget, timeline and quality standards. If something doesn’t go according to plan, you raise the flag early.
You also bring the following knowledge and skills to TNO:
A Master’s degree or PhD in computer science or a related field.
A couple of years (3-5 years) work experience, including some on project management.
Excellent command of both Dutch (C1 level) and English, as you'll work with international teams and Dutch clients.
Preferably experience with IPMA or other project management methodologies.
Demonstrable knowledge and experience in ICT and/or the energy transition is a plus.
Challenging and varied work with a real impact. And plenty of opportunities as, at TNO, you are in charge of shaping your career. We offer a gross monthly salary between € 4.700,- and € 6.800,- (based on your knowledge and experience), 8% holiday pay, a 13th month bonus of 8.33% and a flex budget (5.58% + € 180). In addition, you will be given every opportunity to develop yourself.
TNO offers optional employee benefits, enabling you to tailor your benefits package to match your personal situation. You may also expect:
An extremely professional, innovative working environment where colleagues are leading experts in their field.
The opportunity to attend courses, workshops and conferences, and to receive training and coaching based on your needs.
33 days annual leave on a full-time basis plus a flexible holiday (prorated) that can be used on a cultural, religious, or otherwise important day of your choosing.
An employer that values and encourages diverse talent, with initiatives like the Female Leadership Program, our Rainbow Community and round tables on inclusion topics.

title: Projectmanager ICT | The Hague or Groningen
description:
A powerful digital future in The Netherlands and Europe is unthinkable without research and innovation. Fortunately, TNO is bursting with exiting project plans, which, thanks to you, will actually come to fruition. As a project manager ICT at TNO, you will work at the cutting edge of applied research and pragmatic ICT solutions for the energy domain. Join our enthusiastic group of ICT specialists and take the lead in project teams. Together with companies, universities, research centres and government partners, you’ll help resolve energy transition issues faced by industry and society as a whole!
You will manage multiple research projects simultaneously, ranging from B2B consultancy assignments to multi-year, grant-funded TKI projects (Top Consortia for Knowledge and Innovation). Especially in consortium projects, stakeholder management is key due to the diverse—and sometimes conflicting—interests at play. You safeguard the shared objectives and the overall progress of the project.
Together with your multidisciplinary team, you will develop innovative proof-of-concepts and demonstrators. Your teams work on topics such as modelling of distributed, hybrid energy systems, Digital Twins, adaptive algorithms, (agentic) AI, and more.
Your focus will initially be on Dutch projects, but depending on your interests, you’ll have the chance to move on to European projects.
You will be part of the Intelligent Cyber-physical Systems Engineering (ICSE) research group. From our locations in Groningen and The Hague, more than 35 scientists, researchers, consultants and project managers demonstrate the added value of digital technologies every day. You will also work closely with colleagues across other departments and units.
ICSE offers a vibrant mix of highly educated professionals of different ages, nationalities, genders and experience levels. What unites us is our passion for applied research and our love for an open, informal working atmosphere. And at TNO you’re never on your own: we support each other, collaborate closely and welcome your ideas.
You thrive on managing multidisciplinary research projects. The inherent uncertainties of innovative projects—with outcomes yet unknown—don’t throw you off balance. Inspiring people, coming up with creative solutions and working together are second nature to you.
With your strong communication skills, you’re able to convince your team, clients, partners and other stakeholders. You navigate sensitivities and differing interests with ease. And of course, you do this within budget, timeline and quality standards. If something doesn’t go according to plan, you raise the flag early.
You also bring the following knowledge and skills to TNO:
A Master’s degree or PhD in computer science or a related field.
A couple of years (3-5 years) work experience, including some on project management
Excellent command of both Dutch (C1 level) and English, as you'll work with international teams and Dutch clients.
Preferably experience with IPMA or other project management methodologies.
Demonstrable knowledge and experience in ICT and/or the energy transition is a plus.
Challenging and varied work with a real impact. And plenty of opportunities as, at TNO, you are in charge of shaping your career. We offer a gross monthly salary between € 4.700,- and € 6.800,-(based on your knowledge and experience), 8% holiday pay, a 13th month bonus of 8.33% and a flex budget (5.58% + € 180). In addition, you will be given every opportunity to develop yourself.
TNO offers optional employee benefits, enabling you to tailor your benefits package to match your personal situation. You may also expect:
An extremely professional, innovative working environment where colleagues are leading experts in their field.
The opportunity to attend courses, workshops and conferences, and to receive training and coaching based on your needs.
33 days annual leave on a full-time basis plus a flexible holiday (prorated) that can be used on a cultural, religious, or otherwise important day of your choosing.
An employer that values and encourages diverse talent, with initiatives like the Female Leadership Program, our Rainbow Community and round tables on inclusion topics.

title: Engineer | Software & Hardware Co-Design
description:
At TNO, we’re looking for a driven engineer who thrives at the intersection of hardware and software. You’ll work on intelligent, reconfigurable, and distributed technologies that power next-generation infrastructures. From adaptive control systems to scalable embedded platforms.
Digital systems are becoming more complex, interconnected, and intelligent. At TNO, we design solutions that integrate hardware, software, and control in a seamless way. Bridging the gap between theory and real-world performance. As an Engineer in our team, you’ll contribute to the co-design, prototyping, and integration of complex cyber-physical systems that need to be fast, reliable, and adaptable.
You’ll work closely with experienced researchers and developers on multidisciplinary projects across domains like AI-enhanced embedded systems, edge-cloud computing, and reconfigurable platforms. Your work will often involve contributions to European and national innovation programs, with direct collaboration with industry partners.
You will:
Co-design and prototype hardware/software systems, including embedded and edge-based architectures.
Contribute to distributed computing platforms and their deployment in real environments.
Integrate sensors, networks, control software and cloud/edge infrastructure into working systems.
You’ll also take part in system-level modeling, simulation, and analysis of complex, multi-domain platforms. All while contributing to documentation, reporting, and knowledge-sharing efforts.
You enjoy connecting the dots between hardware and software. You’re curious, hands-on, and like building systems that work. Even under real-world constraints. Whether it's integrating components, prototyping new architectures, or experimenting with reconfigurable platforms, you take pride in making technology smarter and more robust.
You also have:
MSc or PhD (or near completion) in Computer Engineering, Embedded Systems, Systems & Control, or related fields.
Strong grasp of hardware/software co-design principles.
Familiarity with distributed systems and communication protocols.
Experience in systems integration and engineering methodologies.
Solid programming skills (e.g., Python, C/C++, Java, or Rust).
A collaborative mindset and ability to work in interdisciplinary teams.
Bonus points if you also have experience in:
Experience with real-time systems, FPGAs, or neuromorphic/quantum computing.
Knowledge of cyber-physical systems, control theory, or digital twins.
Affinity with AI/ML in embedded or adaptive systems.
Familiarity with system modeling tools (Simulink, SysML, Modelica).
Experience with open-source platforms or toolchains (e.g., ROS, Zephyr, Yocto).
Participation in applied research, open innovation, or tech transfer projects.
Challenging and varied work with a real impact. And plenty of opportunities as, at TNO, you are in charge of shaping your career. We offer a gross monthly salary between € 3,385 and € 5,000 (based on your knowledge and experience), 8% holiday pay, a 13th month bonus of 8.33% and a flex budget (5.58% + € 180). In addition, you will be given every opportunity to develop yourself.
Perhaps you would like to grow your career in the direction of commerce or consultancy? Or would you like to become a project manager? Immerse yourself in sustainable innovations? Or carve out a career as a technical expert?

title: Research Fellow SW Maintenance & Evolution
description:
In this role, you will do applied research at the intersection of software engineering, program analysis, and industrial practice. You will collaborate with partners such as ASML, Canon, Philips, and Vanderlande, following the “industry as a lab” principle: real systems, real constraints, and results that get used. Your work focuses on technologies for software maintenance and evolution of large codebases: program comprehension, reverse engineering, refactoring, and migration. You will build and validate techniques that help engineers understand systems faster, modernize safely, and reduce long-term cost and risk.
Prototype and evaluate methods and tools for program analysis and program comprehension (e.g., static analysis, parsing, lightweight modeling).
Extract and formalize models from software artifacts (source code, build pipelines, logs, and technical documentation) to support migration and refactoring decisions.
Design and test “digital engineering assistants” that support software analysis or modernization, including responsible use of generative AI where relevant.
Work in multidisciplinary project teams and co-create solutions with industrial engineers; present results in workshops and project reviews.
Contribute to research outputs (technical reports, internal tooling, and—where applicable—scientific publications and conference presentations).
You will typically work with existing industrial assets in languages like C++, C#, and Python on Windows and Linux, and you will help teams move towards more maintainable architectures, tooling, and workflows.
MSc or PhD in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Programming Languages, or a related field.
Hands-on programming skills and the ability to work with (and improve) existing codebases; experience with at least one of C++, C#, Java, or Python.
Interest or experience in program analysis, compilers, language engineering, reverse engineering, or software modernization.
Ability to communicate clearly with both researchers and engineers; comfortable presenting results and recommendations.
Team mindset and ownership: you collaborate, deliver, and iterate based on feedback and project constraints.
Experience with compiler tooling, language workbenches, or code-query frameworks (e.g., LLVM/Clang, Roslyn, ANTLR, tree-sitter).
Experience applying static analysis, intelligent parsing, or lightweight modeling to extract structure from source code and documentation.
Experience building research prototypes in Python and transitioning them into maintainable internal tooling.
Knowledge of software engineering processes in industry (CI/CD, testing strategy, architecture, system engineering).
Interest in responsible generative-AI solutions for software engineering (e.g., retrieval-augmented assistance, evaluation, safety).
Challenging and varied work with a real impact. And plenty of opportunities as, at TNO, you are in charge of shaping your career. We offer a gross monthly salary between € 4,621 and € 7,114 (based on your knowledge and experience), 8% holiday pay, a 13th month bonus of 8.33% and a flex budget (5.58% + € 180).
